creator: imp@FreeBSD.org
text: After reading the advice in the change, I think it's wrong. I think we should strongly encourage commit early, commit often, curate with git rebase -i. The suggested workarounds are tedious and error prone. I used to use them and they have @^!#$& me in the past. By committing and rebasing, you have complete control over the end result. If you 'oops' in the merging, then there's no 'checkpoint' to go back to, especially if there are merge conflicts with the method suggested.
